methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Precision AI is a Series A stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $20M to date. The broader sector comprises 8 private peers (including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker) and no direct public comparables.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to data constraints. Stage-Progression triangulation applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, using the $20M total raised as a proxy base. This yields an implied valuation of $40M. A Peer-Median approach was not applicable: while 4 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 met narrow-stage and valuation-tier filters, making sector-wide median comparisons uninformative.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $34M (low) to $46M (median $40M). This range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression output absent peer corroboration. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable Series A peer transactions in Drones & Autonomous, revenue maturity and unit economics not available in this dataset, and the founder team's prior exit track record. The reliance on a single method limits conviction; validation through customer pipeline, competitive positioning, or disclosed peer metrics would strengthen the assessment.
